004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.

006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.assertions;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
021
022import java.io.*;
023import java.lang.reflect.*;
024
025import org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.*;
026import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.*;
027import org.apache.juneau.marshall.*;
028import org.apache.juneau.marshall.cp.*;
029
030/**
031 * Base class for all assertion objects.
032 *
033 * <h5 class='section'>Test Methods:</h5>
034 * <p>
035 * <ul class='javatree'>
036 *    <li>None
037 * </ul>
038 *
039 * <h5 class='section'>Transform Methods:</h5>
040 * <p>
041 * <ul class='javatree'>
042 *    <li>None
043 * </ul>
044 *
045 * <h5 class='section'>Configuration Methods:</h5>
046 * <p>
047 * <ul class='javatree'>
048 *    <li class='jc'>{@link Assertion}
049 *    <ul class='javatreec'>
050 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Assertion#setMsg(String, Object...) setMsg(String, Object...)}
051 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Assertion#setOut(PrintStream) setOut(PrintStream)}
052 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Assertion#setSilent() setSilent()}
053 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Assertion#setStdOut() setStdOut()}
054 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Assertion#setThrowable(Class) setThrowable(Class)}
055 *    </ul>
056 * </ul>
057 *
058 * <ul class='seealso'>
059 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JuneauEcosystemOverview">Juneau Ecosystem Overview</a>
060 * </ul>
061 */
062@SuppressWarnings({
063   "java:S115", // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention (e.g., MSG_parameterCannotBeNull); System.err/out usage is intentional for assertion error output
064   "java:S106", // System.out/err usage acceptable for assertion output
065   "resource" // out field assigned to System.err which is a JVM-managed singleton not owned by this class
066})
067public class Assertion {
068
069   private static final Messages MESSAGES = Messages.of(Assertion.class, "Messages");
070
071   static final String MSG_parameterCannotBeNull = MESSAGES.getString("parameterCannotBeNull");
072   static final String MSG_causedBy = MESSAGES.getString("causedBy");
073
074   /**
075    * Convenience method for getting the array class of the specified element type.
076    *
077    * @param <E> The element type.
078    * @param c The object to get the class name for.
079    * @return The class name for an object.
080    */
081   @SuppressWarnings({
082      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires cast to Class<E[]> for array class
083   })
084   protected static <E> Class<E[]> arrayClass(Class<E> c) {
085      return (Class<E[]>)Array.newInstance(c, 0).getClass();
086   }
087
088   private String msg;
089   private Object[] msgArgs;
090
091   private PrintStream out = System.err;
092
093   private Class<? extends RuntimeException> throwable;
094
095   /**
096    * Constructor used when this assertion is being created from within another assertion.
097    *
098    * @param creator The creator of this assertion.
099    */
100   protected Assertion(Assertion creator) {
101      if (nn(creator)) {
102         this.msg = creator.msg;
103         this.msgArgs = creator.msgArgs;
104         this.out = creator.out;
105         this.throwable = creator.throwable;
106      }
107   }
108
109   /**
110    * Allows you to override the assertion failure message.
111    *
112    * <p>
113    * String can contain <js>"{msg}"</js> to represent the original message.
114    *
115    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
116    * <p class='bjava'>
117    *    <jk>import static</jk> org.apache.juneau.marshall.assertions.Assertions.*;
118    *
119    * <jc>// Throws an assertion with a custom message instead of the default "Value was null."</jc>
120    *    <jsm>assertString</jsm>(<jv>myString</jv>)
121    *       .setMsg(<js>"My string was bad:  {msg}"</js>)
122    *       .isNotNull();
123    * </p>
124    *
125    * @param msg The assertion failure message.
126    * @param args Optional message arguments.
127    * @return This object.
128    */
129   public Assertion setMsg(String msg, Object...args) {
130      this.msg = msg.replace("{msg}", "<<<MSG>>>");
131      this.msgArgs = args;
132      return this;
133   }
134
135   /**
136    * If an error occurs, send the error message to the specified stream instead of STDERR.
137    *
138    * @param value
139    *    The output stream.
140    *    Can be <jk>null</jk> to suppress output.
141    * @return This object.
142    */
143   public Assertion setOut(PrintStream value) {
144      out = value;
145      return this;
146   }
147
148   /**
149    * Suppresses output to STDERR.
150    *
151    * <p>
152    * This is the equivalent to calling <c>out(<jk>null</jk>)</c>.
153    *
154    * @return This object.
155    */
156   public Assertion setSilent() {
157      return setOut(null);
158   }
159
160   /**
161    * If an error occurs, send the error message to STDOUT instead of STDERR.
162    *
163    * @return This object.
164    */
165   public Assertion setStdOut() {
166      return setOut(System.out);
167   }
168
169   /**
170    * If an error occurs, throw this exception instead of the standard {@link AssertionError}.
171    *
172    * <p>
173    * The throwable class must have a public constructor that takes in any of the following parameters:
174    * <ul>
175    *    <li>{@link Throwable} - The caused-by exception (if there is one).
176    *    <li>{@link String} - The assertion failure message.
177    * </ul>
178    *
179    * <p>
180    * If the throwable cannot be instantiated, a {@link RuntimeException} is thrown instead.
181    *
182    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
183    * <p class='bjava'>
184    *    <jk>import static</jk> org.apache.juneau.marshall.assertions.Assertions.*;
185    *
186    * <jc>// Throws a BadRequest instead of an AssertionError if the string is null.</jc>
187    *    <jsm>assertString</jsm>(<jv>myString</jv>)
188    *       .setThrowable(BadRequest.<jk>class</jk>)
189    *       .isNotNull();
190    * </p>
191    *
192    * @param value The new value for this setting.
193    * @return This object.
194    */
195   public Assertion setThrowable(Class<? extends RuntimeException> value) {
196      throwable = value;
197      return this;
198   }
199
200   /**
201    * Creates a new {@link BasicAssertionError}.
202    *
203    * @param msg The message.
204    * @param args The message arguments.
205    * @return A new {@link BasicAssertionError}.
206    */
207   protected BasicAssertionError error(String msg, Object...args) {
208      return error(null, msg, args);
209   }
210
211   /**
212    * Creates a new {@link BasicAssertionError}.
213    *
214    * @param cause Optional caused-by throwable.
215    * @param msg The message.
216    * @param args The message arguments.
217    * @return A new {@link BasicAssertionError}.
218    */
219   protected BasicAssertionError error(Throwable cause, String msg, Object...args) {
220      msg = f(msg, args);
221      if (nn(this.msg))
222         msg = f(this.msg, this.msgArgs).replace("<<<MSG>>>", msg);
223      if (nn(out))
224         out.println(msg);
225      if (nn(throwable)) {
226         try {
227            // @formatter:off
228            throw BeanInstantiator
229               .of(throwable)
230               .addBean(Throwable.class, cause)
231               .addBean(String.class, msg)
232               .addBean(Object[].class, new Object[0])
233               .run();
234         // @formatter:on
235         } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") ExecutableException e) {
236         // If we couldn't create requested exception, just throw a RuntimeException.
237            throw rex(cause, msg);
238         }
239      }
240      return new BasicAssertionError(cause, msg);
241   }
242}
